content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Resolve canonical links and reconcile redirects, sitemap URLs, internal links, content identity, and scope before reporting conflicts.

hints: For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Canonical Signals model · Theory Canonical Signa…, name the owner, authorization, source and emitted revision, Node, TypeScript, URL, robots, unknown value, Fetch, Promise, AbortSignal, browser, accessibility, report, handle, recovery, and native-transfer boundary, Canonical Signals precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Canonical Signals model · Theory Canonical Signa…, trace this concrete input and predict values, errors, blocking, and output before reviewing it: A theory TypeScript team handles case 6012 during a narrow-screen keyboard and screen-reader review. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to reconcile title, canonical, robots directives, hreflang graphs, JSON-LD, and explicit search non-claims; every external value remains unknown until runtime admission produces an owned value; then change one HTML repair, Cheerio mode, selector, unknown value, record schema, link relation, or metadata case and retain rejected evidence. Browser TypeScript uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, Fetch, streams, Cheerio, Playwright, axe-core, files, processes, packages, containers, assistive-technology review, load, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Resolve canonical links and reconcile redirects, sitemap URLs, internal links, content identity, and scope before reporting conflicts.

For Authorized site auditing and TypeScript Defend the Canonical Signals model · Theory Canonical Signa…, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: A canonical tag forces all consumers to merge pages.
